title: Notopleura thesceloantha
intro: Notopleura thesceloantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Notopleura thesceloantha include alkaloids, particularly notopleurine and thesceloanthine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Notopleura thesceloantha is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as tinctures and capsules, which are marketed for their potential health benefits and therapeutic applications.
